title: hieracium leptocladum subsp. rupigenum
intro: Hieracium leptocladum subsp. rupigenum, also known as a type of hawkweed, is a rare, alpine plant species that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating liver and gallbladder issues due to its hepatoprotective and choleretic effects. Its health benefits include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support liver function. Hieracium leptocladum subsp. rupigenum can be found in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, but its use should be carefully considered due to limited scientific research and potential interactions with other medications.
